title: 'In the war between the US and Iran, both sides are convinced they control the clock'
subhed: Tehran believes Trump needs a deal; Washington thinks the Islamic Republic cannot survive without one, explains Alain Frachon in his column for Le Monde.

title: Malian jihadists seek to blockade capital

Anger grows among London's Jewish community as antisemitic attacks surge
subhed: Two men, aged 76 and 34, were stabbed on Wednesday in Golders Green, a neighborhood home to part of London's Jewish community. Police described the assault, which followed a string of recent attacks, as a 'terrorist incident.'

title: Sarkozy hits back with new evidence at appeal trial over alleged Libyan funding
subhed: While the former French president was expected to react on Wednesday to a damning statement by his former associate, Claude Guéant, his lawyers instead submitted figures claiming that half of the sum the Libyans had sent was paid back to them.

title: At the Musée Picasso Paris, Henry Taylor and the emergence of African American modernity
subhed: A hundred works by the artist reflect the influences that shaped part of his journey and capture, with intensity, the lives of individuals he encountered as well as the history of an entire country.

title: 'Let us into Gaza': An open letter from the international press to Israel
subhed: In a letter to the Israeli authorities co-signed by over two dozen media leaders, including Le Monde, the Foreign Press Association urges Israel to reopen access for journalists to the Palestinian territory, which has been closed since October 7, 2023, and to ensure the protection of Gaza-based journalists.

subhed: François Durvye has claimed to have doubled the far-right billionaire Pierre-Edouard Stérin's net worth when he managed his 'family office.' However, several elements have raised doubts over the credibility of his figures, as most of his growth projects never came to fruition.

title: How Le Monde's London correspondent almost became a Reform UK candidate
subhed: After being approached multiple times, Le Monde's London correspondent recounts the questionable recruitment tactics of the far-right party. Her misadventure reflects Reform UK's efforts to try to take London from Labour on May 7.

title: King Charles III urges US not to turn inward in speech to Congress
subhed: While maintaining a consensual tone, the British monarch said an 'unyielding resolve' was 'needed' to defend Ukraine, and reminded his audience of the importance of the rule of law and environmental protection.
